summary: Post-refactor testing of GoSocial SaaS platform. Backend refactored from monolithic server.py (851 lines) into modular route files. All 29 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI fully functional with successful registration, login, dashboard, and navigation flows.
passed_tests: GET /api/auth/me - get current user with valid token
PUT /api/leads/{lead_id} - update lead and verify persistence
DELETE /api/leads/{lead_id} - delete lead and verify removal
PUT /api/settings - update settings and verify persistence
GET /api/billing/subscription - get subscription status with trial info
GET /api/ - API root returns GoSocial API message
Frontend landing page loads correctly with branding and CTAs
Frontend login page loads with Sign In/Sign Up tabs and Google OAuth
User registration via UI redirects to dashboard
Load Demo Data button seeds 20 leads and conversations
All sidebar navigation links work (Dashboard, Leads, CRM, Analytics, Templates, Billing, Settings)
Settings page shows subscription info with trial badge and days remaining
critical_code_review_comments: Backend refactor from 851-line monolith to modular routes is well done - all routes correctly wired
JWT key warning in logs: 'HMAC key is 28 bytes long, below minimum recommended 32 bytes' - consider using longer JWT_SECRET

test_credentials.note: Tests create unique users per run with pattern test_user_{uuid}@gosocial.com

seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed-data creates 20 leads, 8 conversations with messages, and 8 templates
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend was successfully refactored from monolithic server.py to modular route files. All API endpoints remain functional with identical paths. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_gosocial_api.py covers all endpoints. AI features use mocked responses when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is empty but real AI when key is present.
